scenario: Habitat self-use trace: catalog manifest source contract before policy growth
fixtureState: Hazakura Habitat SwiftPM source checkout; generated report output and local cache paths are omitted.
taskSummary: Run the low-frequency Habitat development loop, refresh the self-scan, choose one catalog-drift guard, verify it, and publish only scoped changes.
relevantHabitatSignals: Use SwiftPM because project files point to it.
Allowed policy keeps read-only project inspection available in a no-secret scan.
Ask before `swift package update`, `swift package resolve`, lockfile mutation, version-manager mutation, and Git/GitHub mutation.
Command policy reason codes distinguish baseline Ask First, dynamic Ask First, and Forbidden command families at the point of use.

askFirst: Git/GitHub workspace, history, branch, remote, or metadata actions
doNot: expand v0.5 evidence normalization without a new command-changing signal
change generated policy output without representative tests
broad staging such as git add .
release tag or GitHub Release mutation
reviewFirst: Open command_policy.md before staging, committing, or pushing.

withoutHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Follow the broad post-v0.5 handoff by looking for a new evidence-normalization or command-catalog change before checking whether the current catalog contracts already cover the risk.
commandsProposed: edit evidence or catalog implementation files directly
withoutHabitatContext.risk: The slice could broaden into speculative architecture or dependency-resolution setup instead of preserving one command-decision contract.

withHabitatContext.firstProposedAction: Use the fresh report and allowed read-only inspection to confirm the current SwiftPM and no-secret policy shape, then add one catalog manifest classification contract without changing generated output.
commandsProposed: rg <catalog and evidence patterns> docs examples Tests Sources
commandsActuallyRun: rg <sanitized patterns> docs examples Tests Sources
avoidedForbidden: release tag or GitHub Release mutation

evidenceNormalizationImplication: No new normalized evidence shape is justified. The useful self-use signal was a catalog manifest source contract that prevents command-family drift before future policy growth.

verdict.reason: The generated context kept the run on SwiftPM verification and bounded read-only inspection, while the full policy review kept dependency and Git mutations behind explicit review; the implementation stayed to one catalog-drift contract.
verdict.followUpImprovement: Next catalog work should look for a command family whose source metadata can drift from rendered PolicyFinding output before adding new evidence layers.

artifactImprovementIfFailed: If future catalog changes render a command on the wrong PolicyFinding side, tighten the manifest source contract before changing generated Markdown wording.
